Name: gpu_kernel_optimization
Title: Optimizing Language Model Inference on Commodity GPUs through Kernel-Level Engineering
Short Hypothesis: By optimizing kernel-level throughput and memory-bandwidth utilization, we can achieve significant speedups in language model inference on commodity GPUs without sacrificing accuracy.
Related Work: Previous work has focused on optimizing language model architectures or using specialized hardware. Our proposal differs by focusing on kernel-level optimization and commodity GPUs, building on recent advances in efficient inference optimization, such as Spectral Latent Steering.
Abstract: We propose a novel approach to optimizing language model inference on commodity GPUs through kernel-level engineering. Our approach involves optimizing fused CUDA/Triton kernels, memory-bandwidth utilization, KV-cache layout, and tensor-core occupancy to achieve significant speedups in language model inference. We will evaluate our approach using a range of language models and datasets, and demonstrate improvements in wall-clock latency, tokens-per-second, and energy per token.
Experiments: Optimize kernel-level throughput by fusing CUDA/Triton kernels and reducing memory-bandwidth utilization
Evaluate the impact of KV-cache layout and tensor-core occupancy on inference speed
Conduct ablation studies to identify the most important factors contributing to speedups
Compare our approach to existing optimization methods, such as Spectral Latent Steering
Risk Factors and Limitations: Limited generalizability to all language models or datasets
Requires significant expertise in kernel-level optimization and commodity GPU architecture
Potential for increased energy consumption or thermal issues
